text:
You have come to the ESPN Africa edition, for other ESPN editions, click above.

PARIS -- Belgian triathlete Claire Michel, whowithdrew from the Paris Games mixed relaydays after swimming in the River Seine for the individual event, said Tuesday that E. Coli was not to blame for her illness.

Organizers have been testing the Seine water for elevated levels of the bacteria.

Michel's illness resulted in the Belgian team withdrawing from the triathlon relay. Details of her illness were not provided by Paris 2024 organizers or the Belgium National Olympic Committee, citing the athlete's right to privacy.

"I am gradually doing better and I returned home to Belgium today," Michel wrote Tuesday on Instagram.

"Blood tests showed that I contracted a virus (not E. Coli). After three days of vomiting and diarrhoea, which left me quite empty, on Sunday I ended up needing more significant medical attention at the clinic (in the Olympic Village)."
title: Belgian triathlete Claire Michel: E. Coli not cause of illness - ESPN
description: Belgian triathlete Claire Michel, who withdrew from the Paris Games mixed relay days after swimming in the River Seine for the individual event, said Tuesday that E. Coli was not to blame for her illness.
